PARANOIA 
Here’s some advice for how to get the best Use our ofthe time you've got available to play. Creating Characters For beginning players, rolling up a character and filling out a character sheet can be an in volved and puzzling procedure. You can make ita fot less intimidating by talking them through the process. This can be done in 15 to 20 minutes — less if your players have read through the Player section, Tt might be even better to give your players 
ppregenerated characters. We include a set with each adventure we publish, and you can always rall up a bunch of characters on your ‘own and hand the filledsin sheets to your players. {Using pregenerated characters is'a good idea for two reasons: First, Paranoia characters need personalities that fit the peculiar Paranoia universe, We tailor the abilities and idiosyncrasies of our pregenerated characters to fit the adventure, ‘We design the characters as a team, antici pat ing the charming and homicidal interactions to be expected among Troubleshooting col- Teagues. After studying the pregenerated PCs, you'll see how it's done. Infact, as you design your own adventures you'll be better able to atch your own characters to your twisted dramatic needs, and certainly youl be better able to match your characters tothe strengths, peculiarities, and psychological instabilities of your players Second, using pregenerated PCs isa big help to novice players. They don’t have to master, the intricate details of a new character system right of the bat; instead, they start playing im- mediately.
